channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: GPT 5.4 is a big step for Codex
item.description: On evaluating and understanding the frontier of agents, and why I still turn to Claude.

item.title: Iranian Hackers Likely Used Malware-Stolen Credentials in Stryker Breach
item.description: The medtech giant has been working on restoring systems affected by the cyberattack conducted by the Handala hackers. The post Iranian Hackers Likely Used Malware-Stolen Credentials in Stryker Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: The Pentagon’s new AI plans, and next-gen nuclear reactors
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The Pentagon is planning for AI companies to train on classified data, defense official says  The Pentagon plans to set up secure...

item.title: Apple Debuts Background Security Improvements With Fresh WebKit Patches
item.description: The lightweight updates are meant to deliver security protections between security updates. The post Apple Debuts Background Security Improvements With Fresh WebKit Patches appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Increased errors on Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 18, 12:30 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Claude Code Security and Magecart: Getting the Threat Model Right
item.description: When a Magecart payload hides inside the EXIF data of a dynamically loaded third-party favicon, no repository scanner will catch it – because the malicious code never actually touches your repo. As teams adopt Claude Code Security for static analysis, this is the exact technical boundary where AI...

item.title: 9 Critical IP KVM Flaws Enable Unauthenticated Root Access Across Four Vendors
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have warned about the risks posed by low-cost IP KVM (Keyboard, Video, Mouse over Internet Protocol) devices, which can grant attackers extensive control over compromised hosts. The nine vulnerabilities, discovered by Eclypsium, span four different products from GL-iNet...

item.title: Researcher Discovers 4th WhatsApp View Once Bypass; Meta Won’t Patch
item.description: Meta does not plan on fixing the vulnerability because it involves the use of a modified client application. The post Researcher Discovers 4th WhatsApp View Once Bypass; Meta Won’t Patch appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Product Walkthrough: How Mesh CSMA Reveals and Breaks Attack Paths to Crown Jewels
item.description: Security teams today are not short on tools or data. They are overwhelmed by both.  Yet within the terabytes of alerts, exposures, and misconfigurations – security teams still struggle to understand context:  Q: Which exposures, misconfigurations, and vulnerabilities chain together to...

item.title: HTTP 520 Errors Affecting HEAD Requests
item.description: Mar 18, 09:53 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 18, 09:17 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of HTTP 520 errors, specifically affecting HEAD requests. We are working to analyse and mitigate this problem. More updates to...

item.title: What do new nuclear reactors mean for waste?
item.description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. The way the world currently deals with nuclear waste is as creative as it is varied: Drown it in water pools, encase it...

item.title: Robot Leasing Platform BotShare Raises Nine-Figure RMB Funding in Three Rounds Within Three Months
item.description: BotShare raises $13.8M+ in three months as investors bet on real-world robot deployment.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 18, 07:29 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 18, 06:41 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Geely Reports Record 2025 Revenue of RMB 345.23 Billion, Up 25% YoY
item.description: Geely posts record 2025 revenue of $47.7 billion as vehicle sales surge 39%.

item.title: Tencent Cloud Unveils “Key Sandbox” for AI Agents: Grant Permissions, Not Secrets
item.description: Tencent Cloud launches a “Key Sandbox” to let AI agents operate without ever accessing sensitive credentials.

item.title: Alibaba Cloud Raises AI Computing and Storage Prices by Up to 34%
item.description: Alibaba Cloud raises AI infrastructure prices by up to 34% as demand and token usage surge.

item.title: Tencent Launches Upgraded QClaw AI Agent with Deep WeChat Integration
item.description: Tencent upgrades QClaw with full WeChat mini program integration, pushing AI agents closer to everyday workflows.

item.title: Apple Fixes WebKit Vulnerability Enabling Same-Origin Policy Bypass on iOS and macOS
item.description: Apple on Tuesday released its first round of Background Security Improvements to address a security flaw in WebKit that affects iOS, iPadOS, and macOS. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-20643 (CVSS score: N/A), has been described as a cross-origin issue in WebKit's Navigation API that could be...

item.title: Critical Unpatched Telnetd Flaw (CVE-2026-32746) Enables Unauthenticated Root RCE via Port 23
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security flaw impacting the GNU InetUtils telnet daemon (telnetd) that could be exploited by an unauthenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary code with elevated privileges. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-32746, carries a CVSS score...

item.title: [AINews] Claude Cowork Dispatch: Anthropic's Answer to OpenClaw
item.description: a quiet day.

item.title: Neural-Symbolic Logic Query Answering in Non-Euclidean Space
item.description: arXiv:2603.15633v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Answering complex first-order logic (FOL) queries on knowledge graphs is essential for reasoning. Symbolic methods offer interpretability but struggle with incomplete graphs, while neural approaches generalize better but lack transparency....

item.title: NextMem: Towards Latent Factual Memory for LLM-based Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.15634v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Memory is critical for LLM-based agents to preserve past observations for future decision-making, where factual memory serves as its foundational part. However, existing approaches to constructing factual memory face several limitations. Textual...

item.title: AIDABench: AI Data Analytics Benchmark
item.description: arXiv:2603.15636v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As AI-driven document understanding and processing tools become increasingly prevalent in real-world applications, the need for rigorous evaluation standards has grown increasingly urgent. Existing benchmarks and evaluations often focus on isolated...

item.title: The Comprehension-Gated Agent Economy: A Robustness-First Architecture for AI Economic Agency
item.description: arXiv:2603.15639v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agents are increasingly granted economic agency (executing trades, managing budgets, negotiating contracts, and spawning sub-agents), yet current frameworks gate this agency on capability benchmarks that are empirically uncorrelated with...

item.title: Form Follows Function: Recursive Stem Model
item.description: arXiv:2603.15641v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recursive reasoning models such as Hierarchical Reasoning Model (HRM) and Tiny Recursive Model (TRM) show that small, weight-shared networks can solve compute-heavy and NP puzzles by iteratively refining latent states, but their training typically...

item.title: Tokenization Tradeoffs in Structured EHR Foundation Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.15644v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Foundation models for structured electronic health records (EHRs) are pretrained on longitudinal sequences of timestamped clinical events to learn adaptable patient representations. Tokenization -- how these timelines are converted into discrete model...

item.title: XLinear: Frequency-Enhanced MLP with CrossFilter for Robust Long-Range Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2603.15645v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time series forecasters are widely used across various domains. Among them, MLP (multi-layer perceptron)-based forecasters have been proven to be more robust to noise compared to Transformer-based forecasters. However, MLP struggles to capture complex...

item.title: Alternating Reinforcement Learning with Contextual Rubric Rewards
item.description: arXiv:2603.15646v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement Learning with Rubric Rewards (RLRR) is a framework that extends conventional reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) and verifiable rewards (RLVR) by replacing scalar preference signals with structured, multi-dimensional,...

item.title: Steering Frozen LLMs: Adaptive Social Alignment via Online Prompt Routing
item.description: arXiv:2603.15647v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are typically governed by post-training alignment (e.g., RLHF or DPO), which yields a largely static policy during deployment and inference. However, real-world safety is a full-lifecycle problem: static defenses degrade...

item.title: How to Achieve Prototypical Birth and Death for OOD Detection?
item.description: arXiv:2603.15650v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Out-of-Distribution (OOD) detection is crucial for the secure deployment of machine learning models, and prototype-based learning methods are among the mainstream strategies for achieving OOD detection. Existing prototype-based learning methods...

item.title: Recursive Language Models Meet Uncertainty: The Surprising Effectiveness of Self-Reflective Program Search for Long Context
item.description: arXiv:2603.15653v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-context handling remains a core challenge for language models: even with extended context windows, models often fail to reliably extract, reason over, and use the information across long contexts. Recent works like Recursive Language Models (RLM)...

item.title: MedArena: Comparing LLMs for Medicine-in-the-Wild Clinician Preferences
item.description: arXiv:2603.15677v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly central to clinician workflows, spanning clinical decision support, medical education, and patient communication. However, current evaluation methods for medical LLMs rely heavily on static, templated...

item.title: MiroThinker-1.7 & H1: Towards Heavy-Duty Research Agents via Verification
item.description: arXiv:2603.15726v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present MiroThinker-1.7, a new research agent designed for complex long-horizon reasoning tasks. Building on this foundation, we further introduce MiroThinker-H1, which extends the agent with heavy-duty reasoning capabilities for more reliable...

item.title: Morphemes Without Borders: Evaluating Root-Pattern Morphology in Arabic Tokenizers and LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2603.15773v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work investigates how effectively large language models (LLMs) and their tokenization schemes represent and generate Arabic root-pattern morphology, probing whether they capture genuine morphological structure or rely on surface memorization....

item.title: COGNAC at SemEval-2026 Task 5: LLM Ensembles for Human-Level Word Sense Plausibility Rating in Challenging Narratives
item.description: arXiv:2603.15897v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We describe our system for SemEval-2026 Task 5, which requires rating the plausibility of given word senses of homonyms in short stories on a 5-point Likert scale. Systems are evaluated by the unweighted average of accuracy (within one standard...

item.title: Cloudflare Access experiencing delays with Access app creation and updates
item.description: Mar 18, 02:24 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Mar 18, 02:19 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an issue causing configuration changes for creating, updating, and removing Access applications to be delayed. Existing Access applications are not...

item.title: Nemotron 3 Nano 4B: A Compact Hybrid Model for Efficient Local AI
The Pentagon is planning for AI companies to train on classified data, defense official says
item.description: The Pentagon is discussing plans to set up secure environments for generative AI companies to train military-specific versions of their models on classified data, MIT Technology Review has learned.  AI models like Anthropic’s Claude are already used to answer questions in classified settings;...

item.title: Why Anthropic Thinks AI Should Have Its Own Computer — Felix Rieseberg of Claude Cowork & Claude Code Desktop
item.description: Claude Cowork came out of an accident.

item.title: Cloudflare Workers Issues
item.description: Mar 17, 21:11 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 16, 23:16 UTCMonitoring - We have identified and implemented a fix for this issue. We are currently monitoring the results.Mar 16, 21:41 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of errors for customers running...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Mar 17, 20:02 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 17, 19:47 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: Excel Plugin is inaccessible for some users
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Connectors/Apps (Operational)

item.title: High Error rates with ChatGPT 5.4 Pro
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: State of Open Source on Hugging Face: Spring 2026
Measuring progress toward AGI: A cognitive framework
item.description: We’re introducing a framework to measure progress toward AGI, and launching a Kaggle hackathon to build the relevant evaluations.

item.title: Tech Giants Invest $12.5 Million in Open Source Security
item.description: Anthropic, AWS, Google, Microsoft, and OpenAI fund the Linux Foundation’s long-term security initiatives focused on open source software. The post Tech Giants Invest $12.5 Million in Open Source Security appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 4.6
item.description: Mar 17, 15:45 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Mar 17, 15:28 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Mar 17, 15:21 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.Mar 17, 14:07 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is...

item.title: UK Companies House Exposed Details of Millions of Firms
item.description: The government agency confirmed the vulnerability could have been exploited to obtain company details and alter records. The post UK Companies House Exposed Details of Millions of Firms appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Holotron-12B - High Throughput Computer Use Agent
The Download: OpenAI’s US military deal, and Grok’s CSAM lawsuit
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Where OpenAI’s technology could show up in Iran  OpenAI has controversially agreed to give the Pentagon access to its AI. But where exactly...
